name: Fairmont Field Club
about: The Fairmont Field Club is a traditional country club established in 1912 in Fairmont, West Virginia. It features a challenging 9-hole golf course, a clubhouse, and the 1912 Tavern. The club has expanded its offerings to include modern amenities, such as three indoor golf simulator bays available for member use. Members can access the simulators for practice, play, and private or group lessons. The venue also offers other recreational activities, including pickleball and a seasonal pool, making it a multi-sport facility for its members.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to 3 indoor golf simulator bays for year-round practice and play
Private and group lessons available with PGA Professional Sarah Yost
Part of a comprehensive country club with a 9-hole course and other amenities
Weather-independent practice environment with a focus on improvement

food_and_drink.notes: 1912 Tavern is an on-site bar and restaurant

simulator_hardware.notes: Brand and model not specified, described as 'improvement bays'

faq.question: Do I need clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify if clubs are provided. It is recommended to bring your own clubs or inquire directly with the club.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The website mentions an on-site 1912 Tavern, but does not specify a policy on outside food and drink for simulator sessions. It is best to confirm with the club directly.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the time required to play 18 holes in the simulators.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is family-friendly and offers junior golf programs, a pool with a splash pad and playset, and hosts activities for the entire family throughout the summer.
